Caption:
Hello everyone! Today it’s Studio Ghibli’s turn! I really enjoyed making this drawing since I’m a big fan of Hayao Miyazaki.🫶🏻✨
For this video I thought I’d change things up a bit. I’ve been reading all your comments and you came up with so many great themes, so I decided that from now on I’ll pick the theme randomly each time so I can eventually do them all!
So stay tuned to my profile. ✨

Caption:
Hayao Miyazaki refused to use CGI for a 4-second crowd scene in The Wind Rises.

Instead, animator Eiji Yamamori spent 15 months hand-drawing 96 unique frames — crafting every movement, every gesture, with intention.

Four seconds of film.
Over a year of work.

Why?
Because real craft can't be rushed.
Real art can't be automated.

In a world obsessed with shortcuts —
true mastery is still drawn by hand, one frame at a time.

Follow @cashequation for more insights like this!🤝

Caption:
🔸 Apologies for the speed! Try pausing where necessary to absorb the visual information! 🙏

🔸 We are always told to use references and study the masters, but we are rarely ever taught how! Here is an example I tackled recently, learning from the masters of grass and foliage, Studio Ghibli.✨

🔸 A great place to start is by observing, analysing and replicating your reference. Try to break down the process and understand how the artist arrived to a certain conclusion.🤔

🔸 The next step is to take your learnings and attempt to apply it to your own artwork, putting your newfound revelations to the test! 👀✨

🔸 As artists we are always experimenting and learning. Make studying a part of your practice as an artist, and never stop observing, analysing and being inspired by your fellow artists!💪

AI Ghibli Edits Are Going Viral — But Are They Legal?

The internet is flooded with dreamy, Studio Ghibli-style AI portraits — and yes, they’re beautiful. But behind the trend is a serious legal and ethical gray area that most people aren’t talking about.

When you prompt an AI to “make this look like Ghibli,” you’re not just being inspired by an aesthetic — you’re asking the model to replicate the signature style of a copyrighted visual artist. And if you don’t own the original photo you’re transforming, you may be creating an unauthorized derivative work on top of that.

This trend has become so popular it’s putting a strain on platforms like OpenAI and Midjourney. But the legal system hasn’t caught up — and neither has the public conversation.

It’s worth noting: Ghibli’s founders were outspoken about the value of human-created art. One even called AI-generated art “an insult to life itself.” So while musicians are rightly upset about AI mimicking their voices or styles, why are visual artists treated differently?

We can’t afford to be selective about which art forms deserve protection. Just because something is trending doesn’t mean it’s legally or ethically safe.

Let’s talk about the future of creativity, consent, and copyright in the age of AI.

Caption: The plot twist..#studioghibli #stvlightss #hayaomiyazaki #edit #whenmarniewasthere

May this kind of love find me.

The Wind Rises is remembered for its breathtaking animation and quiet beauty, but at its heart, it’s a love story. Not the loud, dramatic kind, but the kind built on patience, devotion, and choosing each other even when time is painfully limited.

Jiro and Naoko know their future is uncertain, yet they continue to love with a tenderness that makes every shared moment feel precious.

Some films make you believe in soulmates.

The Wind Rises makes you believe that love can be beautiful not because it lasts forever, but because it’s cherished while it lasts.

🎬 The Wind Rises

#thewindrises #ghibli #studioghibli #cinema #love

Caption:
STUDIO GHIBLI part 1

Studio Ghibli almost never existed. They were told animation couldn't be art 🎨
1985: Two animators started a studio that everyone said would fail. They hand-drew every frame while computers took over. They made films for children that treated them like adults. They almost went bankrupt three times.
By 1988, Totoro flopped and they had months before closing forever. Then Princess Mononoke changed everything.
This is the story of how two rebels saved animation by refusing to compromise.
Part 1 of 2 ✨

Caption:
As balas Sakuma Drops, famosas no Japão ganharam maior conhecimento # através do filme Túmulo dos Vagalumes (Hotaru no Haka), um dos clássicos mais lindos do Studio Ghibli. No filme, a lata de balas torna-se um símbolo profundo da luta pela sobrevivência e da inocência perdida durante a Segunda Guerra Mundial. Essas balinhas representaram no filme um raro momento de alegria e conforto em meio à tragédia e ao caos da guerra.
Ainda hoje, as latas de Sakuma Drops são vendidas no Japão, e para muitos, como para mim elas carregam uma forte carga nostálgica e emocional, conectando-os a essa história linda e triste.
O filme é tristíssimo, preparem os lencinhos e me contem o que vcs acharam?!
#hotarunohaka #ghibli #ghiblistudio #tumulodosvagalumes #vanekairijapan #vanekairiculturajapao #japao #japan #tokyo #toquio #sakuma

Caption:
Part 3 of crocheting a Studio Ghibli blanket! We’re now 60% of the way done ✅ 

🌟Graphs are by @☆ JILLY’S ☆ 
🧶 Yarn is mostly Hobbii Amigo in DK/light weight. For Totoro I used black, white, dark beige, gray melange, cast iron, ecru, and dusty green. For Calcifer I used dusty green, sunflower, black, white, and chocolate. I also used Premier DK in pumpkin and orange!

Caption:
🌿🪞Here are some before/after pictures from my current project “The Antique Shop”. A Sylvanian Families customization to spread my love for Studio Ghibli art. 🎥 The new video of the process is now on YT (link in my bio). 

🎨 🏠 I already worked on the building and the furniture, and the next step will be the garden with the huge tree (Totoro Vibes?). The last part will cover the creation of all those little objects that will serve as a tribute to their stories.

Caption:
Hoy quiero hablarte de un tema que está dando mucho de qué hablar: la inteligencia artificial y el arte.

He visto a muchas personas emocionadas por transformar sus fotos en estilos icónicos como el de Studio Ghibli 🎨✨, pero me pregunto… ¿realmente conocemos y valoramos el trabajo de los artistas detrás de estos estilos? 🤔

No se trata de estar en contra del avance tecnológico, porque la IA puede ser una gran herramienta si se usa de manera ética y respetuosa. Pero cuando se usa para replicar el arte de alguien sin su consentimiento, ¿es un homenaje?

Si realmente queremos honrar a los artistas, primero deberíamos conocer su trabajo, respetarlo y entender el esfuerzo que hay detrás. 🖌️💡

¿Qué opinas sobre este tema? Te leo en los comentarios. 💬👇

Hi friends!! ₊˚⊹♡ I am so excited to share something new! These Forest Spirit Wall Hangers will be available on the shop August 18th 1 PM HST 🩷 I wanted to incorporate the kodamas from Princess Mononoke, little totoro, and soot sprites 🌱 These took really long to make but hopefully I will get faster at making them! With that being said, there will only be a handful available but I will definitely be making more!! ✨

These are made with polymer clay and then I double sealed them with uv resin and 2 part resin! (-: Although they took a lot of time and energy im so so proud of them 🥹 Let me know what you think!! <3
